nodes.context: Neo-Nazi terrorist organisation founded in 1992 in the UK, active worldwide.
Neo-Nazi group affiliated with Combat 18, added to Canadian terrorist list alongside it.
Far-right political party that formed Combat 18 as a stewarding group in 1992.
Alleged splinter group of Combat 18, set up by former C18 second-in-command Del O'Connor.
Splinter group from Combat 18 in 2002, retains close links to parent organisation.
Neo-Nazi group that received firearms trafficked by Serbian Combat 18.
Terrorist organization banned in New Zealand in 2025; Combat 18 was instrumental in setting it up.

edges.evidence_quote: On 21 June 2019, the government of Canada added Combat 18 (alongside its affiliate Blood & Honour) to its list of terrorist organisations
edges.context: Blood & Honour is an affiliate of Combat 18.

edges.evidence_quote: In early 1992, the far-right British National Party (BNP) formed Combat 18 as a stewarding group
edges.context: The BNP formed Combat 18 as a stewarding group.

edges.evidence_quote: Some journalists believed that the White Wolves are a C18 splinter group, alleging that the group had been set up by Del O'Connor, the former second-in-command of C18
edges.context: White Wolves are alleged to be a splinter group of Combat 18.

edges.evidence_quote: A group calling itself the Racial Volunteer Force split from C18 in 2002, although it has retained close links to its parent organisation.
edges.context: Racial Volunteer Force split from Combat 18 but retains close links.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2019 Serbian Combat 18 was discovered to be trafficking firearms to Atomwaffen neo-Nazis selling handguns, assault rifles, grenades and RPG-7s from the Balkans to French neo-Nazis in Marseille.
edges.context: Serbian Combat 18 trafficked firearms to Atomwaffen Division.

edges.evidence_quote: The New Zealand Police said in a statement that Combat 18 had been instrumental in setting up the O9A.
edges.context: Combat 18 was instrumental in setting up the Order of Nine Angles.
